23	// Verdict is the outcome of a post-merge bead review.
24	type Verdict string
25	
26	const (
27		// VerdictApprove means all AC items passed; the bead stays closed.
28		VerdictApprove Verdict = "APPROVE"
29		// VerdictRequestChanges means some AC items need fixing; the bead is reopened.
30		VerdictRequestChanges Verdict = "REQUEST_CHANGES"
31		// VerdictBlock means escalation should stop; the bead is flagged for human review.
32		VerdictBlock Verdict = "BLOCK"
33	)

86	// Rule: max(impl_tier + 1, smart). Since smart is the ceiling, the

119	// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
120	// The reviewing agent must produce a single JSON object matching the
121	// ReviewVerdict schema (schema_version: 1). The markdown contract this
122	// replaces silently mis-parsed `### Verdict: APPROVE` outputs whenever the
123	// model echoed the prompt's options header — see review_verdict.go.
124	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
125	
126	For each acceptance-criteria (AC) item, decide whether it is implemented correctly, then assign one overall verdict:
127	
128	- APPROVE — every AC item is fully and correctly implemented.
129	- REQUEST_CHANGES — some AC items are partial or have fixable minor issues.
130	- BLOCK — at least one AC item is not implemented or incorrectly implemented; or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
131	
132	## Required output format (schema_version: 1)
133	
134	Respond with EXACTLY one JSON object as your final response, fenced as a single ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` code block. Do not include any prose outside the fenced block. The JSON must match this schema:
135	
136	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json
137	{
138	  "schema_version": 1,
139	  "verdict": "APPROVE",
140	  "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification",
141	  "findings": [
142	    { "severity": "info", "summary": "what is wrong or notable", "location": "path/to/file.go:42" }
143	  ]
144	}
145	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `
146	
147	Rules:
148	- "verdict" must be exactly one of "APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "BLOCK".
149	- "severity" must be exactly one of "info", "warn", "block".
150	- Output the JSON object inside ONE fenced ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` block. No additional prose, no extra fences, no markdown headings.
151	- Do not echo this template back. Do not write the words APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK anywhere except as the JSON value of the verdict field.`
152	
153	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
154	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
155	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
156	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
157		Caps evidence.Caps
158	}
159	
160	// BuildReviewPromptResult is the structured output of BuildReviewPromptBounded.
161	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled
162	// prompt still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in
163	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
164	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {
165		Prompt   string
166		Overflow bool
167		Sections []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection
168	}
169	
170	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
171	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
172	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
173	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
174		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
175	}
176	
177	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
178	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
179	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
180	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
181	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
182	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
183	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
184	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
185		caps := opts.Caps
186		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
187			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
188		}
189	
190		var sb strings.Builder
191		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
192	
193		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
194	
195		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
196		beadStart := sb.Len()
197		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
198		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
199	
200		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
201			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
202		} else {
203			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
204		}
205	
206		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
207			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
208		} else {
209			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
210		}
211	
212		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
213			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
214		}
215	
216		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
217			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
218		}
219	
220		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
221		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
222			Name:          "bead",
223			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
224			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
225		})
226	
227		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
228		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
229		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
230			invStart := sb.Len()
231			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
232			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
233			for _, f := range allFiles {
234				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
235				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
236			}
237			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
238			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
239				Name:          "changed-files",
240				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
241				SelectedItems: paths,
242			})
243		}
244	
245		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
246		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
247		if len(refs) == 0 {
248			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
249		} else {
250			for _, ref := range refs {
251				if ref.Title != "" {
252					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
253				} else {
254					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
255				}
256				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
257				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
258				if readErr != nil {
259					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
260					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
261						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
262						TruncationReason: "read_error",
263						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
264					})
265				} else {
266					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
267					if truncated {
268						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
269					}
270					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", txt)
271					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
272						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
273						BytesIncluded: len(content),
274						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
275					}
276					if truncated {
277						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
278						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
279					}
280					sections = append(sections, sec)
281				}
282				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
283			}
284		}
285		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
286	
287		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
288		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
289		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
290		diffSection.Name = "diff"
291		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
292		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n"))
293	
294		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
295		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
296		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
297		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
298	
299		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
300	
301		out := sb.String()
302		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
303			Prompt:   out,
304			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
305			Sections: sections,
306		}
307	}
308	
309	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
310	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
311	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
312	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
313	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
314	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
315	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
316		if len(files) == 0 {
317			return originalDiff
318		}
319		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
320			if f.Path == "" {
321				return 3
322			}
323			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
324				return 0
325			}
326			for _, r := range refs {
327				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
328					return 1
329				}
330			}
331			return 2
332		}
333		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
334		copy(ordered, files)
335		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
336	
337		var b2 strings.Builder
338		for _, f := range ordered {
339			body := f.Body
340			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
341				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
342			}
343			b2.WriteString(body)
344		}
345		return b2.String()
346	}
347	
348	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
349	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
350	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
351	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
352	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
353		var b strings.Builder
354		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
355		b.WriteString(f.Path)
356		b.WriteString(" b/")
357		b.WriteString(f.Path)
358		b.WriteString("\n")
359		if f.Stat != "" {
360			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
361			b.WriteString("\n")
362		}
363		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
364			b.WriteString(h)
365			b.WriteString("\n")
366		}
367		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
368		return b.String()
369	}
370	
371	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
372	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
373	// exact symbolic resolution).
374	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
375		if text == "" || p == "" {
376			return false
377		}
378		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
379			return true
380		}
381		base := path.Base(p)
382		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
383	}
384	
385	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
386	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
387	// documents.
388	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
389		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
390			return false
391		}
392		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
393		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
394			return true
395		}
396		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
397			return true
398		}
399		return false
400	}
401	
402	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
403	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
404		var buf bytes.Buffer
405		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
406			"&", "&",
407			"<", "<",
408			">", ">",
409		).Replace(s))
410		return buf.String()
411	}
412	
413	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
414	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
415	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
416	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
417		ProjectRoot string
418		BeadStore   BeadReader
419		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.FizeauService used to dispatch the
420		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
421		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
422		Service agentlib.FizeauService
423		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
424		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
425		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
426		Runner AgentRunner
427		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
428		// When empty, Harness defaults to "claude" and Model is resolved
429		// from TierSmart for the chosen harness.
430		Harness string
431		Model   string
432		// Caps configures the per-section evidence caps used when assembling
433		// the review prompt (FEAT-022). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
434		// applies.
435		Caps evidence.Caps
436	}
437	
438	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
439	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev, implHarness, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
440		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
441		if err != nil {
442			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
443		}
444	
445		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
446		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
447		if err != nil {
448			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
449		}
450	
451		// Resolve governing document references.
452		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
453	
454		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
455		iter := 1
456	
457		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
458		caps := r.Caps
459		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
460			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
461		}
462		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
463		prompt := built.Prompt
464		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
465		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
466		if err != nil {
467			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
468		}
469		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
470			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
471		}
472	
473		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
474		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
475		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
476		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
477		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
478		if built.Overflow {
479			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
480			overflowTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
481				Sections:    built.Sections,
482				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
483				OutputBytes: 0,
484				Harness:     r.Harness,
485				Model:       r.Model,
486			}
487			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
488				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
489				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
490				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
491				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
492				ReviewerModel:   r.Model,
493				BaseRev:         baseRev,
494				ResultRev:       resultRev,
495				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
496				InputBytes:      overflowTelemetry.InputBytes,
497				OutputBytes:     overflowTelemetry.OutputBytes,
498			}
499			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
500				Harness:          r.Harness,
501				Model:            r.Model,
502				BaseRev:          baseRev,
503				ResultRev:        resultRev,
504				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
505				BeadID:           beadID,
506				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
507				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
508			}, reviewArtifactResult{
509				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
510				Error:            evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
511				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
512			})
513			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
514				evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow, len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
515		}
516	
517		// Resolve reviewer harness and model.
518		reviewHarness := r.Harness
519		if reviewHarness == "" {
520			if implHarness != "" {
521				reviewHarness = implHarness
522			} else {
523				reviewHarness = "claude" // default reviewer harness
524			}
525		}
526		reviewModel := r.Model
527		if reviewModel == "" {
528			reviewModel = ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
529		}
530	
531		start := time.Now()
532		runRuntime := AgentRunRuntime{
533			Prompt:          prompt,
534			WorkDir:         r.ProjectRoot,
535			HarnessOverride: reviewHarness,
536			ModelOverride:   reviewModel,
537		}
538		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)
539	
540		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
541		if runErr != nil {
542			// Transport-class failure (FEAT-022 §12): network or provider-side
543			// error. Surface as a typed review-error so the loop classifies and
544			// counts it correctly, rather than masquerading as a BLOCK verdict.
545			transportTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
546				Sections:    built.Sections,
547				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
548				OutputBytes: 0,
549				ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
550				Harness:     reviewHarness,
551				Model:       reviewModel,
552			}
553			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{

data.text:
Commit landed. AC walkthrough:

1. **execute_bead_review.go no longer defaults to implementer harness** — `ReviewBead` resolves `reviewHarness` to `r.Harness` or `"claude"`; `implHarness` is gone. Test: `TestReviewBead_NoLongerDefaultsToImplementerHarness` (execute_bead_review_pairing_test.go:185).

2. **Reviewer call carries Role='reviewer' + same CorrelationID + MinPower=actualPower+1** — `BuildReviewExecuteRequest` (execute_bead_review.go:466) sets `Correlation["role"]="reviewer"`, copies impl correlation, sets `MinPowerOverride = impl.ActualPower + 1`. The reviewer's `dispatchReviewRun` plumbs through `executeOnService`, which honors `MinPowerOverride` (service_run.go:170) and stamps `runtime.Correlation` onto `req.Metadata`. Test: `TestBuildReviewExecuteRequest`.

3. **review-pairing-degraded event emitted when reviewer's RoutingActual.Provider == implementer's** — appended in `ReviewBead` (execute_bead_review.go ~line 753) via `r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent`. Body via `reviewPairingDegradedBody` carries both routing details. Tests: `TestReviewBead_DegradedPath_SameProvider_EmitsEvent` and `TestReviewBead_HappyPath_DifferentProvider_NoDegradedEvent`.

4. **Tests cover happy/degraded/payload** — execute_bead_review_pairing_test.go has all three plus the AC1 regression and a zero-power edge case.
